text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, there was a wise old owl named Oliver. Having lived through many winters, Oliver had a deep understanding of nature's cycles. One day, a pair of young bird siblings, Thistle and Peewee, visited him with a quest.

"My dear Oliver," began Thistle, "we've heard of the Snowy Owl mating for life, but never of birds finding such a partner as you."

Peewee chimed in nervously, "Does it really happen, Uncle Oliver? Will we ever see such a thing?"

Oliver perched on a branch. "Birds look for companionship," he said thoughtfully. "Often, they pair with the same mate from year to year, especially during harsh winters."

"But have you ever tried?" asked Peewee.

"I have," Oliver replied. "Years ago, I was once paired with a fellow owl." He twinkled.

"And?" Peewee asked eagerly.

Oliver leaned forward. "
